seoTitle: How to Sync Bill.com With QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Learn how to connect Bill.com to QuickBooks Online, map clearing accounts correctly, and run your first automated bill sync.
intro: Manually re-entering vendor bills between Bill.com and QuickBooks Online invites duplicate rows and mismatched ledgers. This guide shows how to connect the two platforms through the QuickBooks App Store, set up a dedicated clearing account, and run your first sync. It's built for accounts payable teams who want their payment platform and accounting ledger to stay in step.

steps.heading: Find the Bill.com app
steps.body: From the left sidebar, click Apps, then Find Apps, and search for Bill.com (or BILL) in the marketplace.

steps.heading: Authorize the connection
steps.body: Click Get App Now to launch the secure authorization flow, then log in with your Bill.com credentials and complete any multi-factor prompts.

steps.heading: Map your accounts
steps.body: Review the account mapping screen to pair Bill.com vendor records and expense categories with the correct rows in your Chart of Accounts.

steps.heading: Set up a clearing account
steps.body: Create a dedicated Bill.com clearing account so payment activity doesn't double up against your live bank feed.

steps.heading: Run and verify the first sync
steps.body: Click Sync Now to pull in historical bills and vendors, then check the Expenses directory to confirm everything landed correctly.

faq.question: Why do I need a separate clearing account for Bill.com?
faq.answer: Without one, payments synced from Bill.com can double-count against transactions already pulled in through your live bank feed.

faq.question: Will syncing overwrite my existing QuickBooks vendor list?
faq.answer: The integration is designed to match existing vendors where possible, but it's worth reviewing the mapping screen closely before your first sync.

faq.question: How often does the sync run?
faq.answer: You can trigger a manual sync anytime from the dashboard, and the integration also supports scheduled automatic syncing depending on your settings.

faq.question: What should I do if I see mismatched data after syncing?
faq.answer: Use the Bill.com sync status dashboard inside QuickBooks to locate and troubleshoot specific mismatch errors.
